articles.title: How Trump Purged Immigration Judges to Speed Up Deportations
articles.summary: Judges are ordering an unprecedented number of people deported after coming under significant pressure from the administration to do so or risk losing their jobs.

articles.title: New Deadline Looms for U.S. and Iran as Truce Wavers
articles.summary: Fractures were already emerging in the limited cease-fire. Vice President JD Vance will lead a U.S. delegation in talks this weekend.

articles.title: On Social Media, Democratic Politicians Are Letting the F-Word Fly
articles.summary: Democratic politicians are swearing with glee. It is usually aimed at President Trump.

articles.title: Is the Strait of Hormuz Reopening? What to Know Under Iran-Cease Fire.
articles.summary: Vessels are wary of passing the coast of Iran in the strait, given the fragile agreement, and the number of ships traveling through it has even dropped.

articles.title: Iran-U.S. ceasefire off to a shaky start. And, Bill Gates to testify in Epstein probe
articles.summary: Attacks persist across the Middle East despite the two-week ceasefire deal between the U.S. and Iran. And, Bill Gates is set to testify in the investigation of the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

articles.title: Trump meets with NATO chief about the future of the partnership
articles.summary: President Trump met with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte Wednesday to discuss the future partnership after Trump disparaged the alliance.

articles.title: A ‘Messy’ Cease-Fire, and a 15-Year Sentence for the ‘Ketamine Queen’
articles.summary: Plus, the latest country planning to ban social media for teens.

articles.title: Trump warns strikes will resume if Iran doesn't agree to his peace terms
articles.summary: President Trump said that any peace deal would not allow nuclear enrichment in Iran, and would need to keep the Strait of Hormuz open, as conflicting messages surface over the terms of the ceasefire.

articles.title: Markets Have Faced a Year of Chaos and Still Done Awfully Well
articles.summary: Most stock investors have lost money during the Iran war, but returns have been splendid for the year since the “Liberation Day” tariff announcement.

articles.title: In a Deep Red Town In Pennsylvania, Locals Vent Over a Planned ICE Detention Center
articles.summary: The Tremont, Pa., area has roughly 2,000 residents and limited resources. The Trump administration plans to convert a warehouse there to hold nearly four times as many people.

articles.title: Why high oil prices are good for oil companies — until they aren't
articles.summary: Yes, higher crude oil prices mean a multibillion-dollar cash infusion to the oil industry. But volatility is bad for business, and sustained high prices come with very serious drawbacks.

articles.title: How to make a high-deductible health plan and HSA work for you
articles.summary: If you chose a cheaper health plan, you may be stuck with some hefty medical bills until you meet your deductible. Here's how to get the most out of your plan and health savings account.

articles.title: Morning news brief
articles.summary: Trump and NATO chief meet to discuss the partnership, attacks continue across the Gulf region despite U.S.-Iran ceasefire, Iran says ceasefire includes Lebanon, but Israel and Trump say it's not.

articles.title: Jim Whittaker, the first American to summit Mount Everest, dies at 97
articles.summary: The celebrated mountaineer, who also served as the first full-time employee of the outdoor retailer REI and later as its president and CEO, died Tuesday at his home in Port Townsend, Washington, his f

articles.title: North Korea says its latest weapons tests included missiles with cluster-bomb warheads
articles.summary: North Korea said its testing spree this week involved various new weapons systems, including ballistic missiles armed with cluster-bomb warheads, as it pushes to expand nuclear-capable forces.

articles.title: Guatemalan man pleads guilty in federal court in crash that killed over 50 in Mexico
articles.summary: A man pleaded guilty Wednesday in federal court and acknowledged his involvement in an attempt to illegally smuggle migrants to the U.S. when a truck crashed in Mexico in 2021, killing more than 50.

articles.title: Teen birth rates hit another historical low in 2025, CDC says
articles.summary: The teen birth rate continues its decades-long downward trend. Researchers say many factors are at play, including less sexual activity and more access to contraception and abortion.

articles.title: Once seen as a war hero, Australian ex-soldier will stand trial for alleged war crimes
articles.summary: Until a few years ago, Ben Roberts-Smith was one of Australia's most celebrated war heroes. But now, he will stand trial for alleged war crimes committed in Afghanistan.

articles.title: On Truth Social, Trump Supporters Fume About Iran War
articles.summary: A growing chorus of disaffected Trump supporters is sounding off in the replies to his posts on the social media platform he founded.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
